subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: 2nd pass (2026-05-28, bias-corrected): partner customs (EU/Eurostat, Japan, Korea), diaspora Mandarin (Initium 端傳媒), and Rhodium confirm. Manufacturing strength is real but increasingly a DEPENDENCY (2025 surplus ~57-68% of all growth; ASEAN transshipment escape-valve closing); deflation now 10+ quarters. Trade long and inflation nudged worse; net confirmed, not overturned.

Live-data refresh (2026-05-28): factors re-validated against diverse, independent sources (multilateral bodies + non-US/non-Anglophone press; state-self-reported data still excluded). Notes mark what moved.

v2.0 source-discipline re-run applied. CCP self-reported data (official GDP/CPI, local-government debt, 'stability' framing) EXCLUDED under the hard exclusion rule; economic and near-term-stability factors rebuilt from independent proxies (partner-country customs data, port/freight traffic, electricity, BIS/IIF debt tracking, foreign-firm earnings, independent growth estimates ~2-3% vs official ~5%). Confidence on those factors lowered accordingly.

Trade/industrial capacity re-weighted 0.05 -> 0.17 and scored on INDEPENDENTLY-observable manufacturing dominance (partner customs, port traffic, foreign supply chains). This is a real, externally verifiable strength and lifts the economic score even after CCP-data exclusion.

Slant-balance audit: the prior input set leaned anti-subject on China's strengths (low-confidence CCP) while its negatives were high-confidence independent. Corrected by crediting independently-verified manufacturing capacity AND discounting regime-framed near-term 'stability' positives (political_stability and social_cohesion near-term trimmed, confidence lowered).
NEGATIVE-SKEW case: demographic collapse largely locked in; authoritarian trajectory intensifying; no positive-skew pathway.
Scored on observable ACTIONS and independent DATA over regime stated intentions; the optional strict-foundational-weighting variant was NOT applied.

Recalibrated 2026-05-29 for the 2026 Iran war (Operation Epic Fury, US+Israel vs Iran from 28 Feb) and the Strait of Hormuz closure: blockade of ~1/5 of world oil+LNG, attacks/threats on Gulf desalination (water security), a fragile/violated 8 Apr ceasefire, and the global energy shock. (Reuters; BBC; NYT; Al Jazeera; CSIS; Wikipedia)

economic.notes: Rebuilt under the exclusion rule: CCP official growth/CPI/local-debt figures treated as unusable; factors reconstructed from independent proxies, lowering confidence. Independently-verified manufacturing dominance (now weighted 0.17) is the genuine offset to debt, deflation, captured monetary policy, and property-sector stress. Long-term leans more negative as decoupling and demographics bite.

fiscal_state.notes: Independent (BIS/IIF) non-financial debt ~290-300% GDP; large hidden local-government/LGFV liabilities from collapsed land sales. CCP's own local-debt accounting excluded; proxy estimates used. Debt mostly domestic/RMB so self-restructurable, which caps the tail.

monetary_independence.notes: PBoC is an arm of the Party-state; policy coordination but no institutional check. Structural and externally observable; managing deflation, which is worse than mild inflation in a debt-heavy economy.

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: RMB is the rising dollar-alternative (CIPS, swap lines, trade settlement) - observable via partner-country and BIS data - but capital controls structurally cap true reserve status.

inflation.notes: Imported energy-price shock from the Hormuz closure — China is the largest buyer of Gulf and Iranian crude through the strait — feeds input-cost inflation and a growth drag. (Reuters; BBC)

banking_stability.notes: State-dominated; 'stability through control' masks property-developer defaults and shadow-banking risk. Opaque - confidence held low; inference from observable developer failures and LGFV stress rather than official figures.

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Critical crude-supply disruption (roughly half of China's oil transits Hormuz, and it is Iran's main customer) hits the export-manufacturing base near-term. (Reuters)

institutional.notes: Very weak institutions on the factors weighted most heavily here: no independent judiciary (rule by law, not rule of law), comprehensive censorship, and official statistics unusable for scoring. Built almost entirely from independent sources (V-Dem, WJP, RSF), so confidence is real.

rule_of_law.notes: No independent judiciary; rule BY law not rule OF law; property/contracts contingent on Party tolerance; foreigners face arbitrary action, detention, exit bans.

statistical_integrity.notes: Official statistics unusable (official ~5% vs independent-estimate 2-3% growth); data managed for politics; youth-unemployment series suspended when inconvenient. This finding is the basis for the exclusion rule applied across the economic category.

civil_service_capacity.notes: Genuine technocratic execution capacity, but the claim is partly regime self-presentation, so confidence is lowered; increasingly politicized (loyalty over competence, anti-corruption as control, military purges).

press_freedom.notes: Among the most restrictive globally; comprehensive censorship; no independent media; journalist surveillance/detention.

political_social.notes: Near-term authoritarian 'stability' (suppressed instability) over catastrophic demographics and bottom-tier civil liberties. Regime-framed stability positives trimmed and confidence lowered under the slant audit. Long-term composite materially worse.

political_stability.notes: Trimmed from +2: near-term control is real but the degree of stability is partly regime self-presentation (independently hard to verify). Long-term succession risk (no Xi successor, term limits removed) and authoritarian brittleness.

civil_liberties.notes: Comprehensive surveillance state, social credit, restrictions on speech/assembly/religion (Uyghur persecution), movement; no recourse against the state.

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Structurally closed to immigration; PR extremely rare; no practical citizenship path; ethnonationalist turn; exit-ban risk; rising nationalism.

social_cohesion.notes: Trimmed from +3: low violent crime and surveillance-enforced order are real, but the 'social harmony' framing is partly regime narrative; underlying tensions (ethnic, urban-rural, youth 'lying flat', high youth unemployment).

demographics.notes: 2025 births only ~7.92M (record low); population fell ~3.4M in 2025 (vs 1.4M in 2024) - decline more than doubled; fertility ~1.0; ~60M decline 2026-2035 (independent demographers Yi Fuxian, UNC). Worse and faster than assumed - long to -9.

geopolitical.notes: Few genuine allies; surrounded by wary neighbors; capital controls are a present baseline reality for asset holders, not a tail risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: Few formal alliances (North Korea; Russia partner-of-convenience); transactional/dependency model (BRI); surrounded by wary or hostile neighbors.

conflict_involvement.notes: Visible Taiwan pressure eased: ~169 ADIZ incursions Apr 2026 / 121 Mar - post-2024 monthly lows vs prior ~300+/month; sustained CCG coercion near Kinmen continues. Read as recalibration, not policy change - near nudged to -2; long-term Taiwan/SCS/India risk unchanged at -4.

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Comprehensive capital controls already in place; restricted capital exit; decoupling/secondary-sanction/Taiwan-scenario freeze risk; a baseline reality, not a tail risk.

physical_practical.notes: Genuine, externally observable strengths in infrastructure and (surveillance-derived) street safety; healthcare mixed and aging-strained; the safety positive carries a civil-liberties cost counted elsewhere.

climate.notes: Huge exposure (northern water scarcity, coastal-megacity/delta flooding, heat) offset by top-tier adaptive capacity. (IPCC AR6; WB CCDR)

healthcare.notes: Excellent urban facilities, weaker rural; coverage with gaps and out-of-pocket costs; severe aging strain ahead.

infrastructure.notes: Arguably the world's best new infrastructure: HSR, airports, ports, telecom, smart-city, renewables. Externally observable strength.

crime_safety.notes: Very low violent crime, strong street safety (a surveillance-state artifact); a real quality-of-life positive setting aside the civil-liberties cost.

disaster_insurance.notes: Quake/flood exposure; high state response capacity; developing insurance markets.

summary: Very weak on institutional quality and civil liberties - no independent judiciary, comprehensive censorship and surveillance, structurally closed to immigration - against a largely locked-in demographic collapse. Under the hard exclusion rule, CCP self-reported economics are excluded and rebuilt from independent proxies (lowering confidence); the genuine, externally verifiable strength is manufacturing dominance, now weighted 0.17 and confirmed through partner customs data, which keeps the economic score from collapsing even as structural deflation and decoupling weigh on the long term. The outcome distribution carries negative skew - locked-in demographics and intensifying authoritarianism, with no positive-skew pathway.
